leagueBriefing: Conference tournament season shifted into high gear on Tuesday as Gonzaga claimed another WCC crown, while underdogs like Maryland and Pittsburgh kept their seasons alive with gritty opening-round wins in the Big Ten and ACC.

games.headline: Gonzaga powers past Santa Clara to secure 22nd WCC Tournament title
games.brief: After a sluggish first half that saw them trailing by four, the Bulldogs unleashed a dominant 50-point second half to pull away from the Broncos. Gonzaga's clinical execution down the stretch proved too much for Santa Clara, as the Zags secured their usual spot atop the West Coast Conference and punched their ticket to the Big Dance.
bullets: Gonzaga outscored Santa Clara 50-35 in the second half to erase a halftime deficit.
The victory marks the Bulldogs' 30th win of the season (30-3).
Santa Clara finishes a strong season at 26-8 with a 15-3 conference record.
Gonzaga shot over 55% from the field in the final 20 minutes to seal the game.
games.context: Gonzaga secures the WCC automatic bid, likely cementing a high seed in the NCAA Tournament.

games.headline: Cowboys ride historic scoring night past Buffaloes
games.brief: Oklahoma State's offense clicked like never before in a Big 12 Tournament setting, putting up a program-record 92 points to knock out Colorado. Anthony Roy led the charge with 24 points, but it was the Cowboys' dominance at the free-throw line that truly told the story. Despite Colorado shooting well from deep, they couldn't stop the Cowboys from getting to the rim and the charity stripe, where OSU outscored them by 20 points.
bullets: The 92 points scored by OSU are the most in program history for a Big 12 Tournament game.
Anthony Roy led all scorers with 24 points.
The Cowboys went 29-of-35 from the free-throw line compared to just 9-of-16 for the Buffs.
Colorado's Bangot Dak tied his career-high with 22 points in the defeat.
games.context: Oklahoma State advances to play TCU, while Colorado's season likely ends.

games.headline: Maryland stifles Oregon early to advance in Big Ten Tournament
games.brief: Maryland jumped out to a massive early lead, holding Oregon to just 12 points in the first half. While the Ducks found their rhythm late and put up 48 in the second period, the mountain was simply too high to climb. David Coit led the charge for the Terrapins, who effectively ended Oregon's disappointing season in the tournament opener.
bullets: David Coit led all scorers with 17 points for Maryland.
Oregon's 12 points in the first half was a season low.
Elijah Saunders contributed 15 points to the Terrapins' victory.
The Ducks finish the season with a 12-20 record.
games.context: Maryland advances to the second round of the Big Ten Tournament to face higher-seeded opposition.

games.headline: AJ Dybantsa sets Big 12 record with 40-point masterpiece
games.brief: The AJ Dybantsa show officially arrived in Kansas City. The nation's leading freshman scorer put on an historic performance, dropping 40 points to lead BYU past Kansas State in a high-scoring track meet. BYU's 105 points were the most ever scored in a Big 12 Tournament game, as the Cougars' 'attack-mode' offense proved too much for a Wildcats team that fought valiantly but ultimately ran out of gas against a superstar performance.
bullets: AJ Dybantsa's 40 points set a new Big 12 Tournament freshman scoring record.
BYU's 105 points broke the previous tournament record of 94 set by Kansas in 2001.
The Cougars shot a blistering 54.8% from the field and dominated points in the paint (50-34).
K-State's P.J. Haggerty ended a strong season with 27 points in the loss.
games.context: BYU advances to the second round while K-State's disappointing 12-win season ends.

games.headline: Mustangs charge past Orange behind dominant second half
games.brief: SMU found its rhythm at the perfect time, ending a four-game skid with a dominant second-half performance against Syracuse. After a tight first half, the Mustangs exploded with a 13-0 run sparked by Jaron Pierre Jr.'s lights-out shooting. Syracuse simply couldn't find an answer for the Mustangs' perimeter attack, as SMU cruised into the next round and secured a 20-win season for coach Andy Enfield.
bullets: Boopie Miller led the Mustangs with 25 points, including five triples.
Jaron Pierre Jr. scored 17 of his 21 points in the second half alone.
Jaden Toombs recorded a monster double-double with 16 points and 11 rebounds.
Syracuse struggled from deep, shooting just 26.5% on 34 attempts.
games.context: SMU advances to face Louisville while Syracuse sees their season come to an end.

games.headline: Pittsburgh survives late Stanford surge in ACC Tournament thriller
games.brief: In a classic ACC opening-round grind, Pittsburgh built a lead and then hung on for dear life as Stanford's 40-point second half nearly stole the game. The Panthers' defense did just enough in the final seconds to thwart the Cardinal and keep their slim postseason hopes flickering for at least one more day.
bullets: Stanford outscored Pitt by seven in the second half but fell one point short.
The win marks Pitt's 13th victory in a challenging season.
Stanford finishes the season with a 20-12 record, likely heading to the NIT.
The game featured six lead changes in the final five minutes.
games.context: Pittsburgh advances to face an ACC heavyweight in the next round, while Stanford's bubble officially bursts.

games.headline: Wake Forest outlasts Virginia Tech in high-scoring overtime battle
games.brief: Wake Forest and Virginia Tech traded blows for 40 minutes before the Demon Deacons finally broke away in overtime. In a game defined by offensive sparks and clutch shooting, Wake Forest's 20-point explosion in the extra period proved to be the difference, keeping their ACC Tournament run alive and sending the Hokies home.
bullets: Wake Forest scored 20 points in the five-minute overtime period.
The teams combined for 184 total points in the contest.
Wake Forest improved to 17-15 on the year with the victory.
Virginia Tech ends their season with a 19-13 record.
games.context: Wake Forest moves into the next round of the ACC Tournament, maintaining their slim chances for an at-large bid.

games.headline: McNeese survives triple-overtime marathon to reach Southland Final
games.brief: In arguably the game of the night, McNeese outlasted UTRGV in a grueling three-overtime thriller. Neither team could separate for most of the night, but the Cowboys' resilience in the third extra period finally wore down the Vaqueros. McNeese moves one step closer to an NCAA Tournament berth in dramatic fashion.
bullets: The game required three overtime periods to find a winner.
McNeese improved their impressive record to 27-5.
UTRGV fell despite a valiant 80-point effort in the marathon.
McNeese outscored UTRGV 26-22 across the three overtime periods.
games.context: McNeese advances to the Southland Conference Championship game with an automatic bid on the line.

games.headline: Bears survive FIU scare behind late 3-point barrage
games.brief: Missouri State used a gritty second-half comeback to survive and advance in Huntsville. Trailing by six late in the game, the Bears found their stroke from deep, burying eight 3-pointers in the second half to stun FIU. Kobi Williams hit the go-ahead triple with under two minutes remaining, and the Bears survived a desperation heave at the buzzer to move on to the quarterfinals.
bullets: Kobi Williams led four Bears in double figures with 15 points.
Missouri State shot 53% from the field for the game.
The Bears outscored the Panthers in the final two minutes to erase a late deficit.
FIU's season ends after their final shot at the buzzer went offline.
games.context: Missouri State advances to face top-seeded Liberty in the CUSA quarterfinals.

games.headline: Siena's defensive masterclass secures MAAC title
games.brief: Siena saved its best defensive effort for the biggest game of the year, stifling top-seeded Merrimack to claim the MAAC championship. The Saints held the Warriors scoreless for the first nine minutes of the second half, a dominant stretch that included six blocks and built a lead that even a late Merrimack run couldn't erase. It was a statement win for a Siena team that completely controlled the paint and the pace.
bullets: Siena set a program MAAC Tournament record with 10 blocked shots.
The Saints outscored the Warriors 32-12 in the paint.
Merrimack's star freshman Kevair Kennedy was limited to 15 points.
Siena started the second half on a 12-0 run while holding Merrimack to 0-for-15 shooting.
games.context: Siena earns the MAAC automatic bid to the NCAA Tournament.
